specification.topic-statement: The Google streaming response builder assembles the final response record from state accumulated during a completed streaming session.

scope.in-scope: Final response assembly from streaming context, finish reason override for tool calls, content block construction, thought signature inclusion, and default argument substitution.
scope.boundary: How streaming events are processed and how context state is accumulated (tool ID, name, arguments, thought signature, finish reason, usage) is covered by the Google Streaming Events specification. This specification covers only what happens after all streaming events have been processed.

The HTTP layer detects transfer completion and invokes the completion handler. HTTP status code to error category mapping occurs at that layer. This specification covers the response assembly that occurs within the success path of the completion handler.
The response is delivered to the consumer via the completion callback. This specification covers only how the response is built, not how it is consumed.

field.name: model
field.note: null if not extracted during streaming

field.name: finish reason
field.note: last value from stream; may be UNKNOWN

field.name: current tool id
field.note: non-null if tool call was opened

field.name: current tool name
field.note: non-null if tool call was opened

field.name: current tool thought signature
field.note: last stored signature; prior signatures are discarded

field.name: arguments
field.note: default "{}" when accumulated args are null

behavior.thought-signature-single-slot:
During streaming, when a content part carries both a thoughtSignature field and a functionCall field, the thought signature is stored in a single context slot. If a subsequent part also carries a thoughtSignature with a functionCall, the new value replaces the previously stored value. No history of prior signatures is kept. The final value in the slot at stream completion is the one included in the response.
Thought signatures are not concatenated or accumulated. Each new signature entirely replaces the previous. If multiple function call parts appear with different thought signatures, only the last one survives into the response.
If a part carries a thought signature but is not a function call part, the thought signature is not stored. Only signatures adjacent to function calls are retained.
behavior.response-assembly:
The response is allocated and populated from accumulated streaming state:
The model value from the streaming context is copied into the response. If the streaming context has no model (it was never received), the response model is null.
The accumulated finish reason is copied into the response as the default. This value may then be overridden in the tool-call path (see below).
The accumulated usage counters (input tokens, output tokens, thinking tokens, total tokens) are copied into the response without modification.
The presence of a tool call is determined by checking whether both the tool ID and the tool name in the accumulated state are non-null. If either is null, no tool call block is produced.
When a tool call is detected, the finish reason in the response is overridden to TOOL_USE, regardless of the finish reason that was accumulated from the stream. The content block array is allocated with one element.
The tool call content block is populated: - Type: TOOL_CALL - ID: copied from accumulated tool ID - Name: copied from accumulated tool name - Arguments: copied from accumulated tool arguments string. If the accumulated arguments are null, the arguments field is set to "{}". - Thought signature: copied from the accumulated thought signature slot. If the slot is null, the thought signature field is null.
Even if the Google API returned a "STOP" finish reason in the stream, the response finish reason is unconditionally set to TOOL_USE when a tool call is present in the accumulated state. The streamed finish reason is discarded.
When no tool call is detected (either tool ID or tool name is null), the content blocks field is set to null and the content count is set to zero. The finish reason retains the value accumulated from the stream.
behavior.non-streaming-thought-signature-extracti:
A separate extraction operation exists for non-streaming responses. It searches a complete JSON response for a thought signature in two locations: first at the root level, then within the first candidate object. If a non-empty string is found in either location, it is serialized as a JSON object with a "thought_signature" key and returned as a string. If not found in either location, or if the value is an empty string, null is returned.
This extraction is only invoked for models in the Gemini 3 series.
This extraction path operates on a complete response document, not on streaming context. It is a distinct operation from the per-part signature storage that occurs during streaming.
